file_insights.change: Implemented cmd_resume() from stub to full resumption logic

patterns_discovered.name: directory_enumeration_pattern
patterns_discovered.description: Scans both flat (root/pending) and team-hierarchical (team-*/pending) directory structures
patterns_discovered.implementation: fs::read_dir loop with prefix filtering for team- names

patterns_discovered.name: card_filtering_pattern
patterns_discovered.description: Filters directory entries by extension (.bop or .jobcard) and is_dir() check

patterns_discovered.name: metadata_transaction_pattern
patterns_discovered.description: Read → modify field → write → trigger side effect (thumbnail render)

patterns_discovered.name: error_resilience_pattern
patterns_discovered.description: Continues processing remaining cards on per-card failures; logs warnings with eprintln

patterns_discovered.name: user_feedback_pattern
patterns_discovered.description: Unicode status symbols (▶ for success, ⚠ for warnings) + counters

gotchas_discovered.description: Function signature includes _id parameter (underscore-prefixed) but implementation ignores it; resumes ALL paused cards instead of filtering by ID
gotchas_discovered.implication: API semantic mismatch: cmd_resume() doesn't support single-card resume, only batch resume. User expecting --id flag behavior will be surprised.

gotchas_discovered.description: Two separate empty states: (1) no pending cards found at all, (2) pending cards exist but none are paused. Both print different messages.
gotchas_discovered.implication: Correct behavior but could confuse users about whether cards exist in other states

gotchas_discovered.description: quicklook::render_card_thumbnail() is called but errors are not checked; card is marked resumed even if thumbnail fails
gotchas_discovered.implication: UI will show stale pause status icon until next render

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Card path sorting is done late
gotchas_discovered.description: all_cards collected from multiple directories then sorted; could be expensive if thousands of cards exist
gotchas_discovered.implication: Performance impact negligible for typical workloads

approach_outcome.correctness: Functionally correct for batch resume workflow
approach_outcome.code_quality: Follows established patterns; consistent with pause_single_card style
approach_outcome.test_status: No test changes visible in diff; relies on integration testing

recommendations.suggestion: Clarify whether cmd_resume should support single-card resume (filter by _id) or batch resume (current). If single-card support needed, rename to cmd_resume_all or add --all flag.
Add error handling for quicklook::render_card_thumbnail() call; log failures instead of silent skip
For large card counts, consider lazy-sorting or streaming processing instead of collecting all paths first
Add rustdoc comment explaining batch resume behavior and interaction with pause_single_card
